senses.definition: a writing instrument with an oil-based ink solution and a small metal or plastic ball at the writing tip
a piece of cloth that artists paint on, especially with oil paints

examples.x: The artist stretched a large piece of canvas across a wooden frame, preparing it for an ambitious oil painting that would capture the beauty of the sunset.
During the art class, students learned various techniques for painting on canvas, experimenting with colors and brush strokes to create their own masterpieces.
After weeks of hard work, the painter finally completed her canvas, a vibrant depiction of a bustling city scene that conveyed the energy of urban life.
The gallery featured an exhibition of abstract art, showcasing several large canvases that challenged viewers to interpret their bold shapes and colors.
As he stood in front of the blank canvas, the artist felt a rush of inspiration, eager to translate his emotions onto the fabric with each brushstroke.

senses.definition: a thick, stiff paper used for items like greeting cards, business cards, and other projects that require durable paper
a type of heavyweight, acrylic-coated paper known for its durability and glossy finish
a darkened and filtered pane of glass that was used by landscape painters in the 18th and 19th centuries
a thin, lightweight tissue paper made from shredded paper pulp that is crimped to give it a textured appearance
a tool used for dipping or scooping a quantity of oil from a storage container
a tool used to apply the wet paint film in a consistent and reproducible manner
a special paper that is used by sketchers or draftsmen
a wooden frame, usually a tripod, that an artist uses to support a canvas
a lightweight slanted sketching and drawing board with an angled surface that provides stability for drawing tools while reducing neck strain for artists
a small tool used for removing the marks of a pencil from a piece of paper

examples.x: He gently rubs the eraser over the words to erase them completely.
He lost his eraser and had to ask his friend to borrow one.
I use an eraser to correct my mistakes when writing.
She shares her eraser with her classmates during exams.
They keep a small eraser in their pencil case for quick corrections.

senses.definition: a tool used by artists to protect areas of a drawing or painting while erasing unwanted parts
a metal or plastic ring or cap that fits around the end of a rod or tube to provide reinforcement, protection or to allow it to be attached to a handle
a pen that can be refilled with ink

examples.x: She cherished her grandfather's fountain pen, which he used to write letters during the war.
The CEO signed the contract with a prestigious fountain pen, emphasizing the importance of the agreement.
Fountain pens are favored by writers for their smooth ink flow and ergonomic design.
He filled his fountain pen with blue ink before beginning to write in his leather-bound journal.
The calligrapher demonstrated intricate lettering techniques using a vintage fountain pen.

senses.definition: a pen that uses thick gel ink that dries quickly and allows for smooth strokes with varied shades and colors
an eraser made of synthetic rubber that can be manually shaped and reformed
a jointed model of the human figure that could be put into different positions, used by artists
a type of pen that has a thick tip

examples.x: I keep a set of markers in my art supplies for various art projects.
I use a black marker to write my name on my belongings.
They use washable markers for coloring books and crafts.

senses.definition: a support tool used by artists to steady their hands while drawing or painting
a pencil with a button on top that can be pushed or turned to get more lead out

examples.x: She prefers using a mechanical pencil for writing because it never needs sharpening.
The architect used a mechanical pencil to sketch the initial floor plans of the building.
He keeps a mechanical pencil in his pocket for jotting down quick notes during meetings.
Mechanical pencils come with different lead sizes to accommodate various writing preferences.
The student used a mechanical pencil for the math exam to ensure precise calculations.

senses.definition: the material or substance used by the artist to create the artwork, such as oil, acrylic, watercolor, etc.

examples.x: The artist chose acrylic as the medium for its quick drying time and vibrant colors.
She experimented with different mediums to achieve the desired texture in her painting.
Oil paint is a traditional medium that allows for rich, layered brushstrokes.
Watercolor is a delicate medium known for its transparency and fluid effects.
The sculptor used clay as the medium to shape and mold their intricate designs.

senses.definition: a hand tool used to apply paint to large surfaces quickly and evenly
a tool used to apply paint smoothly and evenly to surfaces
a box containing a set of dry pigments, used by artists in painting
a tool with a flat metal blade used to apply and spread thick layers of oil or paint onto a surface
a spatula with a thin steel blade used for mixing or moving paint by artists
a thin oval board that a painter uses to mix colors and hold pigments on, with a hole for the thumb to go through

examples.x: The artist's palette was covered in a colorful array of oil paints, each hue carefully mixed to capture the essence of the sunset.
She held the palette in one hand and a brush in the other, blending shades of blue and green to create the ocean waves on the canvas.
The painter's palette featured compartments for different colors of acrylic paint, ensuring easy access and organization during the artwork.
He cleaned his palette between painting sessions, scraping off dried paint to prepare it for new color mixtures.
The art student learned how to hold the palette comfortably while practicing color theory and painting techniques in class.

senses.definition: the thin sheets on which one can write, draw, or print things, also used as wrapping material

examples.x: She wrote a letter on a piece of paper and mailed it to her friend.
He drew a beautiful landscape on the blank paper.
The teacher handed out sheets of paper for the students to complete the test.
They wrapped the gift in colorful paper before presenting it.
The printer ran out of paper, so he had to refill it to continue printing.

senses.definition: a thick, sturdy type of paper made by forming several layers of paper pulp into a single sheet
an instrument for writing or drawing with ink, usually made of plastic or metal

examples.x: Can you lend me your red pen to fill out this form?
He writes his thoughts and ideas in a journal with a fancy pen.
She uses a black pen to sign important documents.
They keep a collection of colorful pens for drawing and doodling.
We sign our names with a pen when writing greeting cards.

senses.definition: a tool with a slim piece of wood and a thin, colored part in the middle, that we use for writing or drawing

examples.x: He uses a pencil to shade and create different tones in his artwork.
I use a pencil to sketch and draw.
She lends her pencil to a classmate who forgot to bring one.
She sharpens her pencil before starting her math homework.
We mark important passages in a book with a pencil underline.

senses.definition: a round metal part that rotates in one direction and locks in the opposite direction, used on art tools such as paintbrush handles or adjustable clamps
a coated board that artists cover with white pigment and then scrape lines into using a pointed tool, revealing the contrasting black surface underneath to create high-contrast linear artwork
a flat, blade-like tool used by artists to mix paints, spread materials evenly, and scrape paint from surfaces
a porous material used by artists to apply and blend paints by soaking up paint and releasing it onto a surface
a piece of equipment like a gun that is used to spray a liquid on a surface by an air compressor mechanism
a type of paint that comes in an aerosol can. It is applied to a surface by spraying
a thin metal wire used to assemble and join paper and cardboard pieces into forms and structures
a thin sheet of material with a design or words cut out of it, used to put the design or words on a surface below it by putting paint over
a bar with hooks used to stretch and secure canvas for painting
a rectangular wooden or metal frame used to stretch and secure canvas for painting
a writing or drawing tool used with digital devices and touchscreens
a type of black ink made from soot and glue used in East Asian brush painting
a type of cloth, typically made of cotton, that is coated with a sticky substance such as wax or resin, used to remove dust and dirt from surfaces
a type of writing instrument, typically made of metal, with a fine, pointed tip that is used to draw precise lines and details
any tool used to securely fasten or tighten screws, bolts, or other fasteners, including wrenches and spanners
a type of parchment made from animal skin, typically calfskin, used for writing or printing
a piece of equipment using compressed air that an artist employs to spray paint on a surface
an object that consists of stiff hair or a man-made substitute attached to a handle, used for painting
a brush with bristles spread out in a fan shape, often used for blending colors and creating textured effects like foliage or clouds
a type of paintbrush with tapering bristles that create rounded tip ends
a paintbrush with straight, untapered bristles that produce a straight edge when applied to a surface
a thick, flat brush typically made from goat hair bristles
a specialized type of paint brush used for applying ink
a fine, thin paintbrush used to apply very precise lines and details
a large, roughly textured paintbrush used for applying thick paint in an impasto style
a brush used by a painter to apply paint to a surface

examples.x: The artist dipped the paintbrush into the blue paint and began to create a sky on the canvas.
She used a fine paintbrush to add delicate details to her watercolor painting.
He grabbed a wide paintbrush to quickly cover the wall with a fresh coat of paint.
The paintbrushes of different sizes and shapes were laid out on the table, ready for use.
After finishing the mural, she carefully cleaned her paintbrush to keep it in good condition.

senses.definition: a specialized paintbrush designed for creating very thin, precise lines and patterns
a paintbrush with bristles that taper evenly to form a rounded tip
a tool used by artists to contain and localize materials applied to a surface
a tool used to apply paint or ink in a dotted pattern
a specialized paintbrush used for creating a "drawn-on" or "ragged" style line
